examples.title: Microservice Decomposition with FMEA
examples.description: Decompose a monolithic application into microservices with failure mode analysis.

input.context: Legacy monolithic e-commerce application. 500k LOC Python. Handles catalog, orders, payments, inventory, user auth. Team wants to move to microservices.

expected_output.components_table:
| Component | Bounded Context | Dependencies | Migration Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| Auth Service | User Identity | None (leaf) | 1 [DRV] |
| Catalog Service | Product Data | Auth | 2 [DRV] |
| Order Service | Order Processing | Auth, Catalog, Inventory | 4 [DRV] |
| Payment Service | Financial Txns | Auth, Order | 5 [DRV] |
| Inventory Service | Stock Management | Catalog | 3 [DRV] |
expected_output.fmea_excerpt:
| Component | Failure Mode | S | L | D | RPN | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Payment Service | Network partition during txn | 9 | 4 | 3 | 108 | Saga pattern with compensation [GEN] |
| Order Service | Stale inventory read | 7 | 6 | 5 | 210 | Event-driven inventory sync [GEN] |

examples.title: Design Pattern Selection for Event-Driven System
examples.description: Select appropriate patterns for an event-driven notification system.

input.context: Building notification system for SaaS platform. Must handle email, SMS, push, in-app. Volume: 10M notifications/day. Latency requirement: < 30s for critical alerts.

expected_output.tradeoff_matrix:
| Pattern | Throughput | Latency | Complexity | Reliability | Fit Score |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Fan-out on write | High | Low | Medium | Medium | 7/10 [DRV] |
| Fan-out on read | Medium | High | Low | High | 5/10 [DRV] |
| Priority queue + workers | High | Low (critical) | Medium | High | 9/10 [DRV] |
| Event sourcing + CQRS | Very High | Medium | High | Very High | 6/10 [DRV] |
expected_output.recommendation: Priority queue + workers. Meets latency constraint for critical. At-least-once via SQS FIFO. Complexity manageable for team. [DRV]

examples.title: Cross-Discipline Coordination (Low Energy)
examples.description: Coordinate handoffs between multiple specialist agents for infrastructure migration.

input.context: Migrating CI/CD pipeline from Jenkins to GitHub Actions. Involves DevOps (pipeline), SecOps (secret management), Software Engineer (workflow files), Data Engineer (data pipeline triggers).

expected_output.raci:
| Deliverable | R | A | C | I |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Pipeline config | DevOps | Engineer | Software Eng | SecOps |
| Secret rotation | SecOps | Engineer | DevOps | All |
| Workflow files | Software Eng | Engineer | DevOps | Data Eng |
| Data triggers | Data Eng | Engineer | DevOps | Software Eng |
expected_output.handoff_sequence:
1. Engineer defines scope + constraints [OBS]
2. DevOps drafts pipeline config
3. SecOps reviews secret management
4. Software Eng writes workflow files
5. Data Eng adds data triggers
6. Engineer validates integration [DRV]
expected_output.energy_note: Low energy mode: RACI table + numbered sequence only. No deep analysis. Escalate if blockers found.
